question: A diverging inclusive gateway has three outgoing flows with conditions `=list contains(courses, "pasta")` (Pasta), `=list contains(courses, "steak")` (Steak), and `=list contains(courses, "salad")` (Salad). With `courses = ["pasta", "salad"]`, which flows are taken?

options.text: Only Pasta, since inclusive gateways still select a single flow per token.
All three flows, because the default flow is also taken.
None — the gateway raises an incident when more than one condition is fulfilled.

a.text: The docs state the inclusive gateway takes all outgoing sequence flows whose conditions are fulfilled. Both `pasta` and `salad` are in the list, so both flows are taken.

b.text: Selecting one flow per token is exclusive-gateway behaviour. The inclusive gateway is designed to take multiple paths simultaneously.

c.text: The default flow is only taken when NO condition is fulfilled. Here two conditions are true, so the default is not used.

d.text: Multiple matching conditions are the normal case for an inclusive gateway; no incident is created.

explanation: An inclusive gateway evaluates all outgoing conditions and takes every flow whose condition is fulfilled. The default flow is the fallback only when none match. With `["pasta", "salad"]`, the Pasta and Salad flows are both taken.
